title: inula × magyarica
intro: Inula × magyarica, also known as Hungarian heal-all or golden samphire, is a hybrid plant species within the Asteraceae family, resulting from the cross between Inula helenium and Inula salicina. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content. The health benefits of Inula × magyarica include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The herbal preparations of Inula × magyarica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
